data.title: PayPal's USD Stablecoin PYUSD Launches on Aptos
data.description: PayPal's USD stablecoin, PYUSD, has been expanded to Aptos via LayerZero and Stargate Hydra, launching as PYUSD 0. Aptos is the only Move public chain to support PYUSD 0. Aptos' stablecoin has already surpassed $1.2 billion in value and supports native USDC, USDT, and USDDe, three major global stablecoins. Leveraging LayerZero's cross-chain infrastructure and Stargate interfaces, PYUSD 0 remains fully composable and interoperable on Aptos. This provides the Aptos ecosystem with a new entry point for stable USD. Aptos's inclusion further promotes the adoption of the digital dollar within Web 3 financial infrastructure.

data.title: DEX aggregator Titan completes $7 million seed round led by Galaxy Ventures
data.description: DEX aggregator Titan announced the completion of a $7 million seed round of funding, led by Galaxy Ventures, with participation from Frictionless, Mirana, Ergonia, Auros, Susquehanna, and several angel investors. The company has reportedly completed internal testing of its DEX aggregator, aiming to simplify DEX mechanisms and provide users with more reasonable cryptocurrency quotes. (Blockworks)

data.title: XRP revisits $3 support, but data shows bulls still in control
data.description:
XRP failed to overcome the $3. 20 resistance level, but technical charts and onchain data conclude that bulls are still in control. Is $5 possible by Q4?
source: https://cointelegraph.com/news/xrp-revisits-dollar3-support-but-data-shows-bulls-still-in-control?utm_source=rss_feed&utm_medium=rss&utm_campaign=rss_partner_inbound

data.title: Coinbase Launches USDC On-Chain Lending
data.description: Cryptocurrency exchange Coinbase has announced a new feature that allows users to lend their USDC stablecoin holdings on-chain. The service is powered by the decentralized lending protocol Morpho, with allocations managed through an on-chain vault curated by Steakhouse Financial on Base, the Ethereum Layer 2 network incubated by Coinbase. When users deposit USDC, Coinbase creates a smart contract wallet that routes funds between different lending pools to optimize returns. Coinbase states that users can start earning yields immediately and can withdraw them at any time, depending on liquidity. (The Block)

data.title: XRP Faces Resistance Amid Market Fluctuations
data.description:
According to Cointelegraph, XRP has encountered challenges in maintaining its upward momentum after an 18% rally in early September, which saw the token rise from $2.70 to $3.18. Despite this increase, XRP struggled to surpass the $3.20 mark, facing rejection at a critical resistance level and subsequently falling back to test the $3 support. This setback was exacerbated by the Federal Reserve's recent interest rate cut, which failed to propel XRP above $3.18, leading to continued short-term weakness and another test of the $3 level. Additionally, XRP is finding it difficult to stay above the 50-day simple moving average, contributing to increased selling pressure.

Futures trader DOM noted the importance of the $3.12 level, which bulls failed to hold earlier in the week. This level is crucial for a potential push towards $3.30, with the immediate resistance now set at $3.30. For any significant upward movement, bulls need to reclaim the $3.18 level. Despite these challenges, broader market indicators suggest a sustained bullish outlook for XRP. Onchain data reveals a strong accumulation trend between $2.70 and $3.00, indicating that investors are positioning for potential gains rather than exiting the market. This accumulation is supported by a positive Net Holder position change since August 22, following a period of profit-taking in July and early August.

The Realized Profit/Loss Ratio also highlights a transition phase, with July experiencing the most significant profit-taking of the cycle, leading to a subsequent decline. However, the ratio has recently shown a sharp increase, the strongest since November 2024, suggesting that much of the earlier selling pressure has been absorbed. This, combined with the net holders' accumulation, points to a constructive long-term outlook for XRP. Furthermore, XRP's market fractal pattern remains a bullish indicator. The current setup aligns with the Q1 structure, with the $2.70 low matching the Fibonacci golden pocket. This fractal suggests that XRP is following its expected cycle pattern, potentially setting the stage for a 60–85% rally in Q4, which could see XRP reach the $5.00-$5.50 range from its current level of $3.

data.title: Stablecoin PayPal USD expands to TRON network via LayerZero
data.description: PayPal USD will be listed on the TRON network via the Stargate Hydra platform as a permissionless token, PYUSD0, and will leverage LayerZero’s fully fungible token (OFT) standard, enabling the stablecoin to reach markets and users in need through LayerZero’s distribution network. (The Block)

data.title: Grayscale Prepared to Stake 40,000 $ETH Worth $178 Million: Here is Why This is a Big Deal
data.description:
Grayscale, a prominent digital asset management firm, is positioning itself to stake an impressive total of 40,000 Ethereum (ETH), which is valued at approximately $178 million. This development has significant implications for the cryptocurrency market and the broader investment landscape. Here are several reasons why this move is considered a major event: 

First, Grayscale's decision to stake such a substantial amount of Ethereum highlights their confidence in the long-term potential and stability of this leading cryptocurrency. Additionally, staking ETH not only allows Grayscale to earn rewards through the Ethereum network’s proof-of-stake mechanism but also signals a shift towards a more sustainable and environmentally friendly approach to blockchain technology. 

Moreover, this move may encourage other institutional investors to follow suit, potentially leading to increased demand for Ethereum and contributing to positive price movements. Furthermore, with the cryptocurrency market increasingly gaining mainstream acceptance, Grayscale's stake could be seen as a validation of Ethereum's underlying technology and its applications in decentralized finance (DeFi) and non-fungible tokens (NFTs). 

Ultimately, Grayscale's commitment to staking such a significant amount of ETH represents a strategic decision that could have far-reaching effects on the market, investor sentiment, and the overall evolution of the Ethereum ecosystem.
source: https://blocknews.com/grayscale-prepared-to-stake-40000-eth-worth-178-million-here-is-why-this-is-a-big-deal/

data.title: Hyperliquid's protocol revenue in the past 24 hours surpassed pump.fun
data.description: According to Defillama data, on September 19, Hyperliquid's protocol revenue in the past 24 hours reached US$2.75 million, surpassing pump.fun's US$1.83 million and second only to Tether ($21.83 million) and Circle ($7.75 million).

data.title: PayPal's stablecoin PYUSD0 is now available on the Sei Network
data.description: PayPal's stablecoin, PYUSD 0, has officially launched on the Sei Network, further expanding its cross-chain application scenarios and supporting more on-chain payments and settlements. (Cointelegraph)

data.title: Grayscale Crypto 5 ETF officially listed on NYSE Arca
data.description: Grayscale announced that its CoinDesk Crypto 5 ETF has been listed on the New York Stock Exchange Arca under the ticker symbol GDLC. Formerly known as the Grayscale Digital Large Cap Fund, this multi-asset cryptocurrency ETP covers five major cryptocurrencies: Bitcoin, Ethereum, Solana, XRP, and Cardano. The fund, which is rebalanced quarterly, aims to track the leading assets by market capitalization and liquidity, and is based on the CoinDesk 5 Index developed by CoinDesk Indices.

data.title: U.S. Judge Dismisses Trump's $15 Billion Lawsuit Against The New York Times
data.description:
According to BlockBeats, a U.S. judge has dismissed U.S. President Donald Trump's $15 billion lawsuit against The New York Times, allowing him to amend his complaint. The judge ruled that Trump's filing violated federal rules, requiring a concise statement to justify the plaintiff's entitlement to damages. The judge emphasized that the complaint should not serve as a public forum for insults or a protected platform for attacking opponents.

Previously, on September 16, President Trump filed a defamation lawsuit against The New York Times. He claimed that the publication's reports harmed his reputation, business interests, Truth Social, and his TRUMP cryptocurrency project.

data.title: CFTC Announces New Members for Global Markets Advisory Committee
data.description: According to BlockBeats, the U.S. Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) Acting Chair Caroline D. Pham has announced new members for the Global Markets Advisory Committee (GMAC) and its subcommittees. Scott Lucas, Head of Digital Assets Markets at JPMorgan and Managing Director, has been appointed as Co-Chair of the GMAC Digital Assets Markets Subcommittee. He will serve alongside Sandy Kaul, Executive Vice President at Franklin Templeton.

data.title: Trump says call with Chinese president goes well, will meet during APEC summit
data.description: US President Trump tweeted that he had a "very productive" call with his Chinese counterpart, achieving progress on key issues including trade, the fentanyl issue, the Russia-Ukraine conflict, and the approval of the TikTok transaction. The two sides agreed to meet at the Asia-Pacific Economic Cooperation (APEC) summit in South Korea and plan to exchange visits. Trump called the call "very smooth" and said they would maintain telephone contact and look forward to meeting again at the APEC summit.

data.title: The U.S. Treasury Department has initiated the drafting of the GENIUS Act and is soliciting opinions on stablecoin regulation.
data.description: The U.S. Treasury Department recently launched the Advance Notice of Proposed Rulemaking (ANPRM) process to formally implement the GENIUS Act (U.S. Stablecoin National Innovation Guidance and Establishment Act), signed by President Trump. This marks the beginning of the substantive regulatory phase for stablecoin legislation. The Treasury Department is soliciting comments on details such as issuer restrictions, sanctions compliance, anti-money laundering requirements, the balance between federal and state regulation, tax issues, and custody of reserve assets. The public and crypto companies can submit feedback by October 20th. The GENIUS Act is the first crypto legislation to become law, and the Treasury Department's move aims to further refine the regulatory framework and complement the currently under discussion Digital Asset Market Clarity Act. (CoinDesk)
